name: devflow-file-standards
description: File naming conventions, directory structure, and YAML frontmatter standards for CC-DevFlow. Consolidates shared conventions from all agents.
capabilities: Enforces REQ-XXX format for requirement identification
Standardizes TXXX format for task tracking
Requires YAML frontmatter for PRD, TECH_DESIGN, EPIC, and TASKS files
Standardizes orchestration_status.json schema for workflow tracking
author: Dimon94
securitySummary: This skill contains ONLY documentation about file naming conventions and directory structures. No executable code, network access, or file system operations exist. All 91 static findings are false positives: the scanner misinterprets documentation examples as code, YAML patterns as cryptographic algorithms, and markdown backticks as shell execution.
valueStatement: Inconsistent file naming creates confusion and slows development teams. This skill provides standardized conventions for naming files, organizing directories, and formatting YAML metadata across AI-powered development workflows.
limitations: Only handles naming conventions, not content quality standards
Requires CC-DevFlow workflow system for full integration
Fixed pattern formats cannot be customized per project

useCases.title: Enforce Team Standards
useCases.description: Ensure all team members follow consistent file naming and directory structures across requirements, tasks, and documentation.

useCases.title: Automate File Organization
useCases.description: Integrate standardized naming into AI-driven development workflows for automatic file generation and organization.

useCases.title: Structure Multi-Agent Projects
useCases.description: Define clear file organization patterns for projects involving multiple AI agents and human contributors.

promptTemplates.title: Check File Naming
promptTemplates.prompt: Is 'user-authentication.md' correctly named for a supporting document in CC-DevFlow?

promptTemplates.title: Generate Requirement ID
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a valid REQ-ID for a user authentication feature following the REQ-\\d{3} pattern.

promptTemplates.title: Create Directory Structure
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me the complete directory structure for REQ-123 including all required subdirectories.

promptTemplates.title: Validate YAML Frontmatter
promptTemplates.prompt: What are the required YAML frontmatter fields for a PRD.md file in CC-DevFlow?

bestPractices: Always validate REQ-ID format before creating requirement directories
Use consistent naming patterns across all project documentation
Include YAML frontmatter in all primary documents for metadata tracking
antiPatterns: Mixing naming styles (kebab-case, snake_case, CamelCase) within the same project
Skipping YAML frontmatter on primary documents like PRD.md and EPIC.md
Creating custom naming patterns outside the defined standards

faq.question: Is this compatible with non-CC-DevFlow projects?
faq.answer: Yes, naming conventions can be adopted independently, but full benefits require CC-DevFlow integration.

faq.question: Can I customize the naming patterns?
faq.answer: Patterns are fixed to ensure consistency across workflows. Custom patterns would break automation.

faq.question: How does this integrate with Git?
faq.answer: Standards include Git branch naming like feature/REQ-XXX-{slug-title} for seamless version control.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to use?
faq.answer: This skill only provides documentation. It contains no code that accesses files, network, or credentials.

faq.question: What files require YAML frontmatter?
faq.answer: Primary documents: PRD.md, TECH_DESIGN.md, EPIC.md, and TASKS.md all require frontmatter.

faq.question: How do I validate compliance?
faq.answer: Use the validation scripts in .claude/scripts/ to check file naming before committing changes.
